commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] No narrow automated repair is needed; maintainers need to decide the compatibility and authenticated-Gateway proof gate for this open implementation PR.; Cleared: Cleared: the diff does not add dependencies, workflows, permissions, package hooks, or broader secret storage; credential routing remains env-based, with auth-provider proof handled as merge risk. (sha=012a9f549df90557e19dd288fc987fab158fd3eb)
commands.review_summary:
The PR moves daily and `/stream` cache misses into `DomainDO`, coalesces concurrent generation, hardens stream/fallback storage, separates provider and Gateway credentials, and updates docs, tests, ignore rules, and changelog text.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction path: current main asks the Durable Object for an existing record, then falls through to Worker-side `streamGenerate` on a miss. I did not run a live Wrangler reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] No narrow automated repair is needed; maintainers need to decide the compatibility and authenticated-Gateway proof gate for this open implementation PR.

target.title: [codex] Coalesce daily generation in Durable Object
target.body:
## Summary

- make the per-host Durable Object the sole owner of daily AI generation
- coalesce concurrent `/daily` and `/stream` misses behind one in-flight promise
- keep live streams `no-store` until a complete canonical record is stored
- treat malformed, truncated, timed-out, or disconnected upstream streams as failures and persist deterministic fallback text
- keep Durable Object storage bounded to one record per version, with migration of the current legacy date key
- keep the xAI provider key in `Authorization` and send authenticated Gateway credentials separately in `cf-aig-authorization`
- update regression tests, documentation, and the changelog; preserve contributor credit

## Why

Cloudflare edge cache entries are data-center local, so they cannot enforce the project's one-generation-per-host-per-UTC-day invariant. The Durable Object now owns that invariant. A first `/stream` request can still stream live output, while concurrent requests wait on the same generation and receive only the stored complete record.

The repair also closes operational failure modes in the original patch: caching a stream clone before completion, ending the timeout after response headers, accepting SSE without `[DONE]`, caching a Worker-local fallback that could diverge from Durable Object state, and unbounded date-key storage growth.

## Validation

- `pnpm check` — typecheck, lint, format, and 17 tests
- `pnpm dlx wrangler@4 deploy --dry-run --outdir <temp>` with Wrangler 4.105.0
- real local Wrangler Durable Object runtime with an OpenAI-compatible mock:
  - five concurrent cold requests produced exactly one upstream call
  - live `/stream` and following `/` produced byte-identical canonical output
  - forced midstream disconnect returned an interruption marker to that client, stored a deterministic fallback, and never cached partial output
- exact candidate against real xAI through a counting proxy:
  - five concurrent cold requests produced one authenticated provider call and identical page hashes
  - concurrent live `/stream` and `/` produced one streaming provider call, matching visible text, and the expected `no-store` versus cacheable headers
  - repeated normal requests reused the stored daily record without another provider call
- direct authenticated xAI proof: non-stream and SSE both returned 200; the stream produced content deltas and `[DONE]`; an invalid provider credential was rejected
- three clean `$autoreview` passes: dirty maintainer repair, exact committed branch diff, and the final Gateway-auth repair
- Public Model Identifier Gate: PASS — `grok-4-1-fast-reasoning` is an official xAI alias; xAI documents its redirect behavior in the [May 15 retirement notice](https://docs.x.ai/developers/migration/may-15-retirement)

## Merge gate

Authenticated xAI provider proof passes. Authenticated AI Gateway proof still requires an existing Gateway Run token: the available Wrangler OAuth credential was rejected with 401, no stored Gateway token was available, and no token was created. The implementation now sends provider and Gateway credentials in their documented separate headers. Merge after a token-backed Gateway call or an explicit item-specific proof waiver.
target.author: vincent-peng

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
